behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: A unit test of the prompt-builder alone can miss wiring failures where one collector’s output is dropped or transformed incorrectly before summary generation (MOCK-MISS). Running real module wiring with source adapters stubbed at API boundaries is sufficient; real network/subprocess is not required (REAL-INFRA: no).
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with three controlled source adapters (metrics, issue tracker, deployment log) returning distinct sentinel values, and assert the exact payload handed to the summary step contains all three sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure handling spans source client error mapping plus report assembly. A pure unit mock of one function can miss cross-module exception propagation and section fallback behavior (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network is not strictly needed if the source adapter is made to raise realistic client errors in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline with two sources returning valid data and one source adapter raising timeout/HTTP-unavailable errors; assert report generation completes, includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and process exit is non-fatal.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on semantic faithfulness of generated narrative, which is model-output quality and can vary run-to-run (LLM-DEP). Mocking the generator would hide hallucination/omission failures (MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Call the real summary-generation model on a fixed input dataset with explicit key facts (e.g., counts, trends, incident IDs), evaluate output against factuality checks (must include/must-not-contradict assertions), and run multiple trials to detect variability-driven failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This crosses real external boundaries: APIs plus email transport/inbox delivery (REAL-INFRA). In-process mocks can falsely pass while real auth, TLS, SMTP/API provider, or formatting/inbox rendering fails (MOCK-HIDE). Multiple agentic steps are not required for this assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scheduled-report execution in a test environment against reachable source endpoints and a real email test inbox/provider; verify message is delivered to configured recipients with expected subject/body and attached/generated report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Catching real failure requires exercising transport-level unreachable conditions and persistence across executions. Mocked mail senders often miss real connection/refused/timeout behavior and retry triggers tied to actual run boundaries (MOCK-HIDE, REAL-INFRA).
behaviors.test_description: Run #1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., blocked/invalid SMTP host) and assert report is persisted to local retry storage. Run #2 (next invocation) with email restored; assert the persisted report is retried and delivered, then removed/marked sent.
